yaqub_chosen.title: Yaqub chosen and taught by Allah
yaqub_chosen.introduction: Yaqub (يعقوب عليه السلام), son of the prophet Ishaq and grandson of Ibrahim, is a prophet honored in the Quran. Allah addresses him through his son Yusuf: "Thus your Lord chooses you and teaches you the interpretation of events, and He completes His favor upon you and upon the family of Yaqub, as He completed it upon your two forefathers before, Ibrahim and Ishaq. Indeed, your Lord is Knowing and Wise." (12:6) This verse reveals divine election, prophetic instruction, and the continuity of blessing in the lineage of Ibrahim.
yaqub_chosen.conclusion: Yaqub is thus recognized as a prophet chosen by Allah, instructed in understanding events, and granted divine grace alongside Ibrahim and Ishaq.
yaqub_chosen.moral_lesson: Allah chooses whom He wills and teaches them what He sees fit. The prophetic lineage of Ibrahim is a chain of guidance and mercy.
yaqub_chosen.historical_context: The Quran mentions Yaqub in Surah Yusuf, without detailing his full biography here, but highlighting his election and prophetic instruction.

chapters.title: The divine election of Yaqub
chapters.content: Allah says to Yusuf: "Thus your Lord chooses you (yajtabika) and teaches you the interpretation of events (ta'wil al-ahadith)." (12:6) This same verse applies to the lineage of Yaqub: Allah chose him among His servants and granted him prophetic knowledge. Yaqub belongs to the blessed family of Ibrahim, whom Allah made guides for humanity.

chapters.title: Grace upon Yaqub and his descendants
chapters.content: Allah continues: "And He completes His favor upon you and upon the family of Yaqub (al Ya'qub), as He completed it upon your two forefathers before, Ibrahim and Ishaq. Indeed, your Lord is Knowing and Wise." (12:6) Allah's ni'ma (favor) was manifest across three generations of prophets. Yaqub stands at the center of this blessed descent, father of the prophet Yusuf and heir to the monotheistic mission of Ibrahim.

references.content: وَكَذَٰلِكَ يَجْتَبِيكَ رَبُّكَ وَيُعَلِّمُكَ مِن تَأْوِيلِ ٱلْأَحَادِيثِ وَيُتِمُّ نِعْمَتَهُۥ عَلَيْكَ وَعَلَىٰٓ ءَالِ يَعْقُوبَ كَمَآ أَتَمَّهَا عَلَىٰٓ أَبَوَيْكَ مِن قَبْلُ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ وَإِسْحَٰقَ ۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ
references.translation: Thus your Lord chooses you and teaches you the interpretation of events, and He completes His favor upon you and upon the family of Yaqub, as He completed it upon your two forefathers before, Ibrahim and Ishaq. Indeed, your Lord is Knowing and Wise.
references.relevance: Establishes Yaqub's election, prophetic instruction, and place in the lineage of Ibrahim.

glossary.definition: Prophet, son of Ishaq, grandson of Ibrahim, father of Yusuf.
Divine choice. Allah chose Yaqub among His servants.
Interpretation of events and narratives, taught by Allah.

yaqub_lineage.title: Yaqub in the lineage of Ibrahim
yaqub_lineage.introduction: The Quran places Yaqub at the heart of the blessed descent of Ibrahim. Allah says: "And We gave him Isaac and Jacob. And all [of them] We made righteous." (21:72) Yaqub is thus linked to the prophet Ibrahim through his father Ishaq, and counted among the salihin (righteous) whom the Quran honors.
yaqub_lineage.conclusion: Yaqub is part of the prophetic lineage of Ibrahim. Allah granted him Ishaq as a father and counted him among the righteous, a sign of His grace upon this family.
yaqub_lineage.moral_lesson: Faith is transmitted from prophet to prophet. Yaqub continues the mission of Ibrahim and Ishaq in worshiping Allah alone.
yaqub_lineage.historical_context: The Quran presents Yaqub as son of Ishaq and grandson of Ibrahim, without specifying geographical details of his life here.

chapters.title: Son of Ishaq, grandson of Ibrahim
chapters.content: Allah says about Ibrahim: "And We gave him Isaac and Jacob." (21:72) Ishaq is the father of Yaqub, and Ibrahim is their common ancestor. This triple mention — Ibrahim, Ishaq, Yaqub — shows the continuity of divine blessing upon the lineage of Ibrahim, whom Allah made guides for humanity.

chapters.title: Among the righteous
chapters.content: Allah continues: "And all [of them] We made righteous (salihin)." (21:72) The term salih denotes one who is pious, upright, and obedient to Allah. Yaqub is counted among the salihin alongside his father Ishaq. This prophetic virtue prepares the blessed descent from which Allah will make the prophet Yusuf.

references.content: وَوَهَبْنَا لَهُۥٓ إِسْحَٰقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ ۖ نَافِلَةً ۖ وَكُلًّا جَعَلْنَا صَـٰلِحِينَ
references.translation: And We gave him Isaac and Jacob. And all [of them] We made righteous.
references.relevance: Places Yaqub in the lineage of Ibrahim and counts him among the salihin.

glossary.definition: Prophet, son of Ibrahim, father of Yaqub.
Righteous, pious. Quality attributed to Yaqub in the Quran.
The family of Ibrahim. Yaqub is part of it.

yaqub_patience.title: Yaqub's patience in trial
yaqub_patience.introduction: The Quran relates Yaqub's deep trial when separated from his son Yusuf. His grief was immense, but his response reveals prophetic greatness: he complained only to Allah. "He said: I only complain of my suffering and grief to Allah." (12:86) Yaqub embodies sabr (patience) and trust in Allah, without losing hope in His mercy.
yaqub_patience.conclusion: Yaqub teaches us that a prophet may suffer deeply, but entrusts his pain to Allah alone. His patience and trust in divine mercy remain a model for believers.
yaqub_patience.moral_lesson: Sincere complaint to Allah and patience in trial are prophetic qualities. Never despair of Allah's mercy.
yaqub_patience.historical_context: The Quran mentions Yaqub's grief in Surah Yusuf, emphasizing his patience and trust in Allah rather than retelling the full story of Yusuf.

chapters.title: Deep grief, complaint to Allah alone
chapters.content: Allah says: "And he turned away from them and said: Alas, my grief for Yusuf! And his eyes whitened from grief, and he was of that repressed." (12:84) Facing this pain, Yaqub declared: "I only complain of my suffering and grief to Allah. And I know from Allah that which you do not know." (12:86) The prophet felt immense pain, but he knew Allah held a wisdom that people did not know.

chapters.title: Never despair of Allah's mercy
chapters.content: Yaqub said to his sons: "O my sons, go and inquire about Yusuf and his brother. And do not despair of relief from Allah. Indeed, no one despairs of relief from Allah except the disbelieving people." (12:87) Despite years of separation and grief, Yaqub kept hope in Allah. His sabr (patience) and tawakkul (trust) illustrate prophetic strength in trial.

references.content: قَالَ إِنَّمَآ أَشْكُوا۟ بَثِّى وَحُزْنِىٓ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ وَأَعْلَمُ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ
references.translation: He said: I only complain of my suffering and grief to Allah. And I know from Allah that which you do not know.
references.relevance: Shows that Yaqub entrusted his pain to Allah alone.

references.content: يَـٰبَنِىَّ ٱذْهَبُوا۟ فَتَحَسَّسُوا۟ مِن يُوسُفَ وَأَخِيهِ وَلَا تَا۟يْ‍َٔسُوا۟ مِن رَّوْحِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ لَا يَا۟يْ‍َٔسُ مِن رَّوْحِ ٱللَّهِ إِلَّا ٱلْقَوْمُ ٱلْكَـٰفِرُونَ
references.translation: O my sons, go and inquire about Yusuf and his brother. And do not despair of relief from Allah. Indeed, no one despairs of relief from Allah except the disbelieving people.
references.relevance: Affirms Yaqub's hope and trust in divine mercy.

glossary.definition: Patience, endurance. Central quality of Yaqub in trial.
Complaint. Yaqub complained only to Allah.
Mercy of Allah. Yaqub exhorts never to despair of it.

yaqub_testament.title: Yaqub's testament on tawhid
yaqub_testament.introduction: The Quran relates that Yaqub, like Ibrahim before him, exhorted his sons to remain Muslims — submitted to Allah. "And Abraham instructed his sons [to do the same] and [so did] Jacob, [saying]: O my sons, indeed Allah has chosen for you this religion, so do not die except while you are Muslims." (2:132) As death approached, Yaqub asked his sons about their future worship, confirming tawhid.
yaqub_testament.conclusion: Yaqub transmits the same mission as Ibrahim: worship of Allah alone. His testament is an eternal reminder of monotheism for his descendants and for all believers.
yaqub_testament.moral_lesson: Transmitting tawhid to one's descendants is a prophetic duty. Yaqub teaches us to die in submission to Allah.
yaqub_testament.historical_context: The Quran presents Yaqub's testament as a continuation of Ibrahim's, without adding unrevealed details.

chapters.title: Die only as Muslims
chapters.content: Allah says: "And Abraham instructed his sons [to do the same] and [so did] Jacob: O my sons, indeed Allah has chosen for you this religion (hadha ad-din). So do not die except while you are Muslims." (2:132) Yaqub continued Ibrahim's call to tawhid. He reminded his sons that the religion chosen by Allah is Islam — exclusive submission to Him.

chapters.title: Whom will you worship after me?
chapters.content: Allah asks: "Or were you witnesses when death approached Jacob, when he said to his sons: What will you worship after me? They said: We will worship your God and the God of your fathers, Abraham and Ishmael and Isaac — one God. And we are Muslims [in submission] to Him." (2:133) The sons of Yaqub confirmed pure monotheism. Yaqub, heir to Ibrahim's mission, ensured tawhid would be transmitted to his descendants.

references.content: وَوَصَّىٰ بِهَآ إِبْرَٰهِيمُ بَنِيهِ وَيَعْقُوبُ يَـٰبَنِىَّ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ ٱصْطَفَىٰ لَكُمْ هَـٰذَا ٱلدِّينَ فَلَا تَمُوتُنَّ إِلَّا وَأَنتُم مُّسْلِمُونَ
references.translation: And Abraham instructed his sons [to do the same] and [so did] Jacob: O my sons, indeed Allah has chosen for you this religion, so do not die except while you are Muslims.
references.relevance: Yaqub's testament on maintaining Islam and tawhid.

references.content: أَمْ كُنتُمْ شُهَدَآءَ إِذْ حَضَرَ يَعْقُوبَ ٱلْمَوْتُ قَالَ مَا تَعْبُدُونَ مِنۢ بَعْدِى قَالُوا۟ نَعْبُدُ إِلَـٰهَكَ وَإِلَـٰهَ أَبَآئِكَ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ وَإِسْمَـٰعِيلَ وَإِسْحَٰقَ إِلَـٰهًا وَٰحِدًا وَنَحْنُ لَهُۥ مُسْلِمُونَ
references.translation: Or were you witnesses when death approached Jacob, when he said to his sons: What will you worship after me? They said: We will worship your God and the God of your fathers, Abraham and Ishmael and Isaac — one God. And we are Muslims [in submission] to Him.
references.relevance: Confirms tawhid transmitted by Yaqub to his descendants.

glossary.definition: Testament, recommendation. Yaqub exhorted his sons to tawhid.
Oneness of Allah. Central message of Yaqub's testament.
One submitted to Allah. Yaqub exhorted his sons to die as Muslims.

lessons_from_yaqub.title: Lessons from the prophet Yaqub
lessons_from_yaqub.introduction: The Quran mentions Yaqub in several key verses: divine election, lineage of Ibrahim, patience in trial, and testament on tawhid. Reflecting on Surah Yusuf (12:6, 12:86-87), Al-Anbiya (21:72), and Al-Baqarah (2:132-133), the believer draws lasting lessons on the prophetic mission.
lessons_from_yaqub.conclusion: Yaqub remains a model of divine election, virtue, patience, and transmission of tawhid. His story in the Quran invites trust in Allah and perseverance in faith.
lessons_from_yaqub.moral_lesson: Adhering to authentic texts protects the believer. Yaqub's qualities — election, virtue, patience, tawhid — are goals for every pious soul.

chapters.title: Rely on the Quran
chapters.content: Allah mentions Yaqub in precise, authentic verses. The Muslim honors Yaqub through what Allah said: chosen and instructed (12:6), righteous in the lineage of Ibrahim (21:72), patient in trial (12:86-87), transmitter of tawhid (2:132-133). This is a sound method: knowing the prophets through revelation, without inventing narratives.

chapters.title: Aim for prophetic qualities
chapters.content: Divine election reminds us that Allah guides whom He wills. Virtue in the lineage of Ibrahim invites continuing monotheistic faith. Yaqub's patience teaches entrusting one's pain to Allah alone. The testament on tawhid reminds us to transmit Islam to our descendants. By following these teachings, the believer walks in the footsteps of the salihin, among whom Allah counts Yaqub.

references.content: وَكَذَٰلِكَ يَجْتَبِيكَ رَبُّكَ وَيُعَلِّمُكَ مِن تَأْوِيلِ ٱلْأَحَادِيثِ وَيُتِمُّ نِعْمَتَهُۥ عَلَيْكَ وَعَلَىٰٓ ءَالِ يَعْقُوبَ كَمَآ أَتَمَّهَا عَلَىٰٓ أَبَوَيْكَ مِن قَبْلُ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ وَإِسْحَٰقَ ۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ
references.translation: Thus your Lord chooses you and teaches you the interpretation of events, and He completes His favor upon you and upon the family of Yaqub, as He completed it upon your two forefathers before, Ibrahim and Ishaq.
references.relevance: Summary of the Quranic honors granted to Yaqub.

references.content: وَوَصَّىٰ بِهَآ إِبْرَٰهِيمُ بَنِيهِ وَيَعْقُوبُ يَـٰبَنِىَّ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ ٱصْطَفَىٰ لَكُمْ هَـٰذَا ٱلدِّينَ فَلَا تَمُوتُنَّ إِلَّا وَأَنتُم مُّسْلِمُونَ ۝ أَمْ كُنتُمْ شُهَدَآءَ إِذْ حَضَرَ يَعْقُوبَ ٱلْمَوْتُ قَالَ مَا تَعْبُدُونَ مِنۢ بَعْدِى قَالُوا۟ نَعْبُدُ إِلَـٰهَكَ وَإِلَـٰهَ أَبَآئِكَ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ وَإِسْمَـٰعِيلَ وَإِسْحَٰقَ إِلَٰهًا وَٰحِدًا وَنَحْنُ لَهُۥ مُسْلِمُونَ
references.translation: And Abraham instructed his sons [to do the same] and [so did] Jacob: O my sons, indeed Allah has chosen for you this religion, so do not die except while you are Muslims. Or were you witnesses when death approached Jacob, when he said to his sons: What will you worship after me? They said: We will worship your God and the God of your fathers, Abraham and Ishmael and Isaac — one God. And we are Muslims [in submission] to Him.
references.relevance: Recalls Yaqub's testament on tawhid and transmission of faith.

glossary.definition: Oneness of Allah. Message transmitted by Yaqub to his descendants.
The righteous. Yaqub is among them according to the Quran.
